coach_notes: While graphic design skills are not typically core to hospitality, visual merchandising and branding are highly valuable for roles in hotel marketing, event planning, or luxury service management. Start by taking a free online course in visual merchandising or branding basics on platforms like Coursera or Skillshare, and practice by redesigning a menu or event flyer for a local venue. Your existing customer service and event operations experience gives you a strong foundation—adding these visual skills will make you stand out for leadership roles that blend hospitality with creative presentation.

jobs.title: Customer Service Lead
jobs.label: ready
jobs.why: Strong customer service, leadership, and teamwork skills align well with leading a customer service team. Experience in fast-paced environments and event support demonstrates ability to handle high-volume interactions.
jobs.gaps: No explicit supervisory experience listed; may need to develop skills in performance management or conflict resolution among team members.

jobs.title: Front Desk Agent
jobs.label: target
jobs.why: Customer service and reservations skills are directly applicable to front desk duties. Organization and teamwork support managing guest check-ins and inquiries.
jobs.gaps: No direct hotel front desk experience. May need training on property management systems and handling guest complaints in a hospitality setting.

jobs.title: Deli Baker
jobs.label: reach
jobs.why: Food safety standards and sanitation knowledge are relevant to a deli bakery environment. Teamwork and ability to work in fast-paced settings support production tasks.
jobs.gaps: No baking or deli-specific experience. Lacks skills in dough preparation, baking techniques, and product handling. Significant training needed.

jobs.title: Entry Level Kitchen Position, Murray Hill
jobs.label: target
jobs.why: Food safety standards, sanitation, and experience in a fast-paced kitchen environment are directly applicable. Event support and catering experience show familiarity with kitchen operations.
jobs.gaps: No specific entry-level kitchen experience listed. May need to learn specific kitchen protocols, knife skills, or station organization for this particular establishment.

jobs.title: Barista - Temporary
jobs.label: reach
jobs.why: Customer service skills and ability to work in fast-paced environments are relevant. Cashier experience and handling customer orders translate to barista tasks.
jobs.gaps: No barista or coffee-making experience. Lacks knowledge of espresso machines, drink recipes, and coffee brewing techniques. Significant training required.
